title: Recursive structures: detection
analysis:
Task 203 requires detecting self-referential structure definitions during compilation. Structures in Reify can contain `sub` declarations that reference other structures (e.g., `sub x = B()`). When structure A contains a sub of type B, and B contains a sub of type A (or transitively through any chain), we have a recursive cycle.

The compiler already has all the infrastructure needed: (1) TopologyTemplate has `sub_components: Vec<SubComponentDecl>` where each SubComponentDecl has a `structure_name` field identifying the referenced structure. (2) After all templates are compiled (line 1894), a template_registry HashMap<String, &TopologyTemplate> is built for deferred checks. (3) module_dag.rs provides a pattern for DFS cycle detection.

Approach: Add a post-compilation pass (after all templates are compiled, similar to bound checks) that builds a directed graph of structure references from sub_components, then runs DFS to detect cycles. When a cycle is found, emit a diagnostic and tag the participating templates with `is_recursive = true`. This flag will be consumed by downstream tasks for special handling (e.g., preventing infinite elaboration in reify-eval).

The reference graph is: for each TopologyTemplate T, for each SubComponentDecl S in T.sub_components, add edge T.name -> S.structure_name. Then run DFS with gray/black coloring to find back-edges indicating cycles. When a back-edge is found, extract the cycle path from the DFS stack.

Key design choice: Tag templates with `is_recursive: bool` rather than blocking compilation. Recursive structures may be valid with collection subs or guarded subs — they need special handling at elaboration time, not outright rejection. The diagnostic is a warning-level note identifying the cycle path.

--- REVIEW FIX (reviewer_robustness/incorrect_cycle_detection) ---

The DFS-based cycle detection has a bug: the `Some(false)` arm (black/fully-explored node) takes no action, so when a second DFS path reaches a cycle member that's already fully explored, intermediate nodes on that path are never tagged in_cycle. Example: graph A→B, A→D, B→C, D→C, C→A. DFS finds A→B→C→A and marks A,B,C in_cycle. Then A visits D; D visits C (black, in_cycle), but the `Some(false)` arm does nothing — D is never tagged, yet D participates in the genuine cycle A→D→C→A.

Naive fix (propagate in_cycle when black neighbor is in_cycle) would cause false positives: if X→Y→X is a cycle and Z→Y exists, Z would be wrongly marked even though there's no path Y→Z.

Correct fix: replace the DFS with Tarjan's SCC algorithm. Tarjan's finds all strongly connected components in O(V+E). Every node in an SCC of size > 1 is a cycle participant. Self-loops (SCC of size 1 with self-edge) are also cycles. This handles multi-path convergence correctly because SCC membership is determined by mutual reachability, not by DFS tree structure. Cycle paths for diagnostics can be reconstructed from the SCC's adjacency sub-graph.

steps.description: Write failing test: direct self-reference (structure A has sub x = A()) is detected and tagged as recursive — assert template.is_recursive == true and a warning diagnostic contains 'recursive structure cycle'

Add `is_recursive: bool` field to TopologyTemplate (default false). Add `detect_recursive_structures()` function that builds adjacency list from templates' sub_components and runs DFS cycle detection. Wire it into compile() after all templates are compiled. Set is_recursive=true on templates in cycles. Emit warning diagnostic with cycle path.
Write failing test: mutual recursion (A has sub b = B(), B has sub a = A()) — both A and B are tagged as is_recursive=true
Ensure detect_recursive_structures handles mutual/indirect cycles by propagating is_recursive to all nodes in any cycle, not just the back-edge node
Write failing test: indirect cycle through 3 structures (A->B->C->A) — all three tagged as is_recursive=true and diagnostic message contains full cycle path 'A -> B -> C -> A'
Ensure cycle path reconstruction works for chains of arbitrary length by tracking the DFS path stack and extracting the cycle when a back edge is found
Write failing test: non-recursive structures (A->B, C->D, no cycles) — none tagged as recursive, no warning diagnostics emitted about recursion
Verify non-recursive DAG structures produce no false positives (should already pass if DFS is correct, fix if not)

Write failing test: mixed recursive and non-recursive structures in same module — only cycle participants are tagged is_recursive=true, others remain false. E.g., A->B->A cycle plus standalone C->D — C and D stay is_recursive=false
Ensure tagging is precise: only structures participating in a cycle get is_recursive=true. Structures that reference a recursive structure but aren't themselves in a cycle remain false.
Write failing test: sub referencing unknown/external structure name does not cause panic or false positive — structure with sub pointing to non-existent type compiles without recursion diagnostics
Ensure detect_recursive_structures only adds edges for structure_names that exist in the template set — skip unknown/external references gracefully

Write failing test: structure with multiple sub declarations, one creating a cycle and one not — structure is correctly tagged as recursive. E.g., A has sub b = B() and sub c = C(), B has sub a = A() — A and B are recursive, C is not
Verify all edges from a template are considered (multiple subs), and the diagnostic label points to the specific sub creating the back-edge
Write test: structure with no subs at all is not tagged as recursive and produces no recursion diagnostics
Verify edge case: templates with empty sub_components are handled correctly (no edges added, no iteration issues)

Write failing test for multi-path cycle convergence (reviewer bug): graph A→B, A→D, B→C, D→C, C→A. All four structures participate in cycles (A→B→C→A and A→D→C→A). Assert all four are tagged is_recursive=true. This test will FAIL with the current DFS because D is reached via a black (already-explored) node C and the Some(false) arm takes no action, leaving D untagged.

Replace dfs_detect_cycle with Tarjan's SCC algorithm. Tarjan's correctly finds all strongly connected components in O(V+E) by tracking index and lowlink values on a stack. Tag all nodes in SCCs of size > 1 as in_cycle. Also tag self-loop SCCs (size 1 where node has edge to itself). Reconstruct one representative cycle path per SCC from the adjacency sub-graph for diagnostic messages. Remove the old dfs_detect_cycle function entirely.

Write test for false-positive resistance: structure Z has sub a = A(), and A→B→A is a cycle, but there is no path from A or B back to Z. Assert Z is NOT tagged is_recursive (Z merely points into a cycle without being part of one). This validates that Tarjan's SCC correctly excludes nodes that have edges into an SCC but aren't members of it.

Verify the false-positive test passes with Tarjan's SCC (should pass because SCC membership requires mutual reachability — Z can reach A but A cannot reach Z, so Z is in its own singleton SCC with no self-edge).

Write tests for annotated entity fixtures: trait_structure_module() returns CompiledModule with 1 trait_def ("Rigid" requiring thickness:Length), 1 template ("Plate" with thickness param), 0 diagnostics. field_module() returns module with 1 field (analytical), 1 template. purpose_module() returns module with 1 compiled_purpose, 1 template. Assert expected counts and key field values.

Implement annotated entity fixtures in fixtures.rs using the new builders: trait_structure_module()->CompiledModule (CompiledTraitBuilder::new("Rigid").require_param("thickness",Type::length()).build() + TopologyTemplateBuilder::new("Plate") with thickness param). field_module()->CompiledModule (CompiledFieldBuilder::new("temp",..).analytical(lambda).build() + template). purpose_module()->CompiledModule (CompiledPurposeBuilder::new("mfg_ready").param("subject","Structure").constraint(..).build() + template).

design_decisions.decision: Add `is_recursive: bool` field to TopologyTemplate rather than a separate data structure
design_decisions.rationale: TopologyTemplate is the compiled representation consumed by reify-eval. Tagging it directly makes the information immediately available during elaboration without requiring a side-channel lookup. The field is cheap (1 byte) and zero-cost when false.

design_decisions.decision: Use warning-level diagnostics, not errors, for recursive cycles
design_decisions.rationale: Recursive structures are not necessarily invalid — they may be meaningful with collection subs (bounded count) or guarded subs (conditional inclusion). The task says 'tag for special handling', not 'reject'. Downstream elaboration can decide how to handle them (e.g., depth limits, lazy expansion).

design_decisions.decision: Implement detection as a post-compilation pass rather than inline during compile_entity
design_decisions.rationale: Following the existing pattern of deferred bound checks: all templates must be compiled before we can build the full reference graph. Forward references are allowed within a module, so inline detection during entity compilation would miss structures defined later in the file.

design_decisions.decision: Replace DFS gray/black cycle detection with Tarjan's SCC algorithm
design_decisions.rationale: The original DFS approach misses cycle participants reachable through already-explored (black) nodes. When multiple paths converge on a cycle, the DFS only finds members on the first path. Tarjan's SCC correctly identifies ALL nodes in ALL strongly connected components in O(V+E) by tracking index/lowlink values, handling multi-path convergence without the early-exit issue. Cycle paths for diagnostics are reconstructed from each SCC's adjacency sub-graph.

design_decisions.decision: Place all tests in a dedicated integration test file rather than inline unit tests
design_decisions.rationale: Follows the established codebase pattern where each feature area has its own test file in tests/ (e.g., collection_sub_tests.rs, trait_bounds_tests.rs). The tests need the compile + check diagnostics pattern that integration tests use.

design_decisions.decision: Only consider sub_components for reference graph edges, not type_args
design_decisions.rationale: Type arguments (e.g., Box<Bolt>) reference structures but don't create containment relationships that cause infinite elaboration. A Box<T> parameterized by T doesn't contain T as a sub — it's a type parameter. Recursion through sub containment is what causes structural cycles.

design_decisions.decision: Rejected naive propagation fix (mark in_cycle when black neighbor is in_cycle) in favor of Tarjan's SCC
design_decisions.rationale: Naive propagation causes false positives: if Z→A where A is in cycle A→B→A, Z would be wrongly tagged because its black neighbor A is in_cycle, even though there's no path from A back to Z. Tarjan's SCC correctly requires mutual reachability for cycle membership.

reuse.what: DFS cycle detection pattern from module_dag.rs
reuse.how: Adapt the in_progress HashSet + early return pattern for structure reference graph traversal, extending it with path tracking to report cycle chains
Add the recursive structure detection pass after the existing deferred bound check pass, using the same template_registry HashMap<String, &TopologyTemplate>
Use Diagnostic::warning() with DiagnosticLabel::new(span, message) pointing to the sub declarations that form the cycle

Reuse the same pattern: parse source, assert no parse errors, compile, return CompiledModule. Create a compile_module() helper that allows warnings (since recursion detection emits warnings, not errors).
Use structure_name from each SubComponentDecl to build the adjacency list edges in the reference graph
Iterate compiled templates to extract (template.name -> sub.structure_name) edges for graph construction

Implement iterative Tarjan's with index counter, lowlink array, and explicit stack. Each SCC of size > 1 (or size 1 with self-edge) maps to a set of in_cycle nodes. Reconstruct one cycle path per SCC by walking adjacency within the SCC.
